header.title: Music
header.lead: Music has always been an integral part of our spiritual and cultural life at UU Wellesley Hills.​

intro:
Music has long been a cherished component of worship at UU Wellesley Hills. Each week, a range of music complements and enhances the service. Organ, piano, and choral music make up the bulk of our Sunday offerings, supplemented by guest instrumentalists and several artists-in-residence. Our Sanctuary has superb acoustics, which enhance artists’ performances during Sunday worship and many special events.
​
You need not be a member of the congregation to participate in our music program. All singers and instrumentalists are welcome.

items.title: Choir
items.text:
Rehearses Wednesdays 7:00–8:45pm and Sundays at 9:00am (three Sundays a month). New voices always welcome.


UU Wellesley Hills has a rich choral heritage. Our choir sings a broad range of repertoire, drawn from the last ten centuries and representing a variety of cultures, languages, and musical styles. Rehearsals are designed to be engaging, challenging, and enjoyable – a respite from your busy week. People of high-school age and older are welcome, regardless of experience.
 
We sing for services most Sundays, with the support of our professional section leaders.

items.title: Choir 101
items.text:
Choir 101 is offered every October or by request.

This course, for people of high school age and older, covers basic music terminology and symbols, and gives less-experienced singers a chance to explore their voices in a small group setting. 
 
Choir 101 can be a stepping stone to joining Adult Choir, though by no means a requirement.

items.title: Community Messiah Sing
items.text:
The Community Messiah Sing is an annual December event hosted by UUWH.


All ages from the community and congregation are invited to our Sanctuary to enjoy this seasonal classic, whether singing along or listening.  Entry is always free, though donations are greatly appreciated.
 
The performance features professional soloists and a chamber orchestra, with UUWH Music Director Suzie Cartreine conducting and playing harpsichord.

items.title: Concert Series
items.text:
Building on its rich musical tradition, UUWH launched a Concert Series in Fall 2023.


Building on its rich musical tradition, UU Wellesley Hills launched a Concert Series in Fall 2023.  The programs bring diverse musicians into our space, outside of the structure of Sunday morning services.  We welcome UU Wellesley Hills members, friends, and the wider community to enjoy music of many genres, featuring both local and traveling performers.  Plans are taking shape for our next “season,” with several events in the works.  Recent offerings have included a “roots-infused” coffee house concert, a Celtic music duo, an evening of a capella singing, and a bluegrass band.

artist.heading: Artist in Residence
artist.name: Leanne Rabesa
artist.text: A Boston-based freelance violist, arranger, and composer who enriches our worship and concerts throughout the year.

contact.heading: Get Involved
contact.text: Interested in singing or playing with us? We'd love to hear from you.
